ui-mono.purpose: All interface text — search bar, hover labels, fallback icon letters.

omega-display.purpose: Single-character omega glyph. The only Serif element in the interface.

motion.name: drift-rotation
motion.purpose: Ambient orbital drift. Paused during drag. Disabled when launcher hidden. Respects prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: fps-adaptive
motion.purpose: Aggressive CPU savings when launcher not shown.

components.name: Search Bar (Placeholder)
components.description: Full-width telemetry input panel with mechanical left-edge strikethrough.
components.css: .ds-search-bar { background: rgba(15,15,18,0.85); width: 420px; height: 40px; border: 1px solid rgba(230,25,25,0.40); border-radius: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; padding: 0 16px; position: relative; font-family: monospace; } .ds-search-bar::before { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 0; top: 0; width: 2px; height: 100%; background: rgba(230,25,25,0.70); } .ds-search-label { color: rgba(100,100,105,0.50); font-size: 14px; font-family: monospace; }

components.name: Search Bar (Active Query)
components.description: Search bar with active query and blinking cursor block.
components.css: .ds-search-bar { background: rgba(15,15,18,0.85); width: 420px; height: 40px; border: 1px solid rgba(230,25,25,0.40); border-radius: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; padding: 0 16px; position: relative; font-family: monospace; } .ds-search-bar::before { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 0; top: 0; width: 2px; height: 100%; background: rgba(230,25,25,0.70); } .ds-search-prefix, .ds-search-suffix { color: rgba(234,234,234,0.90); font-size: 14px; font-family: monospace; } .ds-search-query { color: rgba(234,234,234,0.90); font-size: 14px; font-family: monospace; font-weight: bold; } .ds-search-cursor { display: inline-block; width: 7px; height: 16px; background: rgba(230,25,25,0.70); margin-left: 4px; animation: ds-blink 1s step-end infinite; } @keyframes ds-blink { 50% { opacity: 0; } }

components.name: Hover Label
components.description: Telemetry tag shown below hovered icon — ASCII-framed with terminal prompt prefix.
components.css: .ds-hover-label { background: rgba(15,15,18,0.85); border: 1px solid rgba(230,25,25,0.40); border-radius: 0; padding: 5px 8px; font-family: monospace; font-weight: bold; font-size: 14px; color: rgba(234,234,234,0.95); position: relative; display: inline-block; } .ds-hover-label::before { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 0; top: 0; width: 2px; height: 100%; background: rgba(230,25,25,0.70); }

components.name: Fallback Icon Badge
components.description: Square mechanical badge for apps without a resolvable icon. Letter initial with accent left-edge and corner notch.
components.css: .ds-fallback-icon { width: 44px; height: 44px; background: rgba(15,15,18,0.45); border-radius: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; position: relative; font-family: monospace; } .ds-fallback-icon::before { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 2px; top: 2px; width: 3px; height: calc(100% - 4px); background: rgba(230,25,25,0.85); } .ds-fallback-letter { color: rgba(234,234,234,1.0); font-size: 20px; font-weight: bold; font-family: monospace; } .ds-fallback-notch { position: absolute; top: 2px; right: 2px; width: 8px; height: 8px; border-top: 1.5px solid rgba(230,25,25,0.60); border-right: 1.5px solid rgba(230,25,25,0.60); }

components.name: Omega Centerpiece
components.description: Central anchor — serif omega glyph in aviation red, or custom PNG image.
components.css: .ds-omega { font-family: serif; font-weight: bold; font-size: 220px; color: rgba(230,25,25,0.80); line-height: 1; user-select: none; }

components.name: Keyboard Focus Indicator
components.description: Four corner brackets marking the keyboard-focused icon. Aviation red, 2px stroke.
components.css: .ds-focus-indicator { width: 52px; height: 52px; position: relative; } .ds-focus-indicator span { position: absolute; width: 12px; height: 12px; border-color: rgba(230,25,25,0.80); border-style: solid; } .ds-focus-tl { top: 0; left: 0; border-width: 2px 0 0 2px; } .ds-focus-tr { top: 0; right: 0; border-width: 2px 2px 0 0; } .ds-focus-br { bottom: 0; right: 0; border-width: 0 2px 2px 0; } .ds-focus-bl { bottom: 0; left: 0; border-width: 0 0 2px 2px; }

components.name: Icon Satellite (Normal)
components.description: A single application icon satellite — red monochrome recolor on void background.
components.css: .ds-icon-satellite { width: 44px; height: 44px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-icon-placeholder { width: 36px; height: 36px; background: rgba(230,25,25,0.60); border-radius: 0; }

narrative.overview: Orbital Launcher is a single-purpose Linux desktop launcher rendered as a 3D spherical icon cloud. Its visual language draws from military terminal displays, aviation instrument panels, and declassified engineering documents — raw, honest, structurally expressed. Every visual element is justified by function; decoration that doesn't serve the task is prohibited. The interface lives in a dark void (#0A0A0A — the color of a deactivated CRT phosphor screen, not pure black). A single surgical accent (aviation hazard red, #E61919) marks structural edges, the central omega anchor, and the keyboard focus indicator. Text is exclusively monospace, set against the void with no intermediate surfaces except the elevated panel (#0F0F12). Depth is conveyed through alpha falloff, perspective scale, and painter's-sort layering — without wireframes, orbital path rings, or surface textures. The sphere is felt, not drawn.
keyCharacteristics: Single accent color (aviation red) used at ≤15% of any frame
Zero border-radius — every rectangle has sharp mechanical corners
Monospace typography throughout; Serif reserved for the omega glyph only
Depth expressed through alpha + scale + layering, never shadows
CRT textural overlays (noise grain, scanlines) available but off by default
No settings UI — all tuning is code-level configuration

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Aviation red is the only accent color permitted in the interface. Terminal green is a status-only signal reserved for future use. No other saturated colors appear — the desktop icons themselves are recolored to red monochrome unless they come from a recognized red theme.

rules.name: The No-Round Rule
rules.body: Every rectangle, border, and panel has 0px border-radius. Sharp corners are the default and only option. If a corner appears rounded, it's a bug.

rules.name: The Monospace Mandate
rules.body: All UI text — labels, search, fallback letters, telemetry — must be monospace. Serif is reserved exclusively for the omega glyph. Sans-serif fonts are prohibited.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No element casts a shadow. The interface is flat at rest. The only depth cues are the three projective cues: alpha falloff, perspective scale, and painter's sort. If an element needs to read as elevated, use the Elevated Panel background color — nothing else.

dos: Do use the Elevated Panel (#0F0F12) for any surface that must read above the void
Do use aviation red (#E61919) exclusively for edges, borders, the omega, and focus — never as a surface fill
Do keep all corners sharp (0px radius) on every rectangle and panel
Do use monospace for all UI text; Serif is reserved for the omega glyph only
Do convey depth through alpha falloff, perspective scale, and draw order — never shadows
Do keep the interface to four elements: icons, omega, search bar, hover label
Do let the void (#0A0A0A) dominate — the interface is sparse by design
donts: Don't use translucent frosted glass or blur effects (glassmorphism)
Don't use rounded corners — not on cards, not on buttons, not on inputs. 0px radius everywhere
Don't use colorful icon-grid layouts (macOS Launchpad, GNOME app grid)
Don't render skeuomorphic 3D planet textures or orbital path rings — the sphere is implied, not drawn
Don't add superfluous connecting lines or decorative geometry
Don't add gradient text, box shadows, or repeating-linear-gradient stripes
Don't introduce a second accent color — terminal green is reserved for future status use only
Don't add settings UI, category filters, or configuration panels — the launcher does one thing
Don't use sans-serif fonts anywhere in the interface
